description: Deliver a working password manager with encrypted local storage. The user can set a master password, unlock the vault, and perform full CRUD on LoginEntry and SecureNoteEntry. No cloud sync. Covers: shared types, crypto module, vault module, storage layer, background service worker, popup UI, and E2E smoke tests. Scope: Weeks 4–6.
title: Phase 2 — Core Vault

description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs nx lint and nx build browser-extension on every push and PR. Ensures the repo never has a broken build merged to main. Can be extended later with nx affected to only run on changed projects.
title: Add GitHub Actions CI (lint + build on push/PR)

close_reason: Removed Nx boilerplate (src/app, src/main.tsx, index.html). Wired webextension-polyfill into background service worker. nx build succeeds — popup, background, content, and manifest.json all output to dist/. Extension is loadable as an unpacked Chrome extension.
description: Create a minimal manifest.json targeting MV3 with a popup entry. Install webextension-polyfill for Chrome/Firefox API normalization. Build a minimal React popup (popup/index.tsx) that renders 'Hello World'. Acceptance criteria: nx build browser-extension succeeds and loading the dist/ as an unpacked extension in Chrome shows the Hello World popup.
title: Add MV3 manifest, webextension-polyfill, and Hello World popup

close_reason: Implemented crypto.ts with Argon2id (hash-wasm, hashLength=32) + PBKDF2 fallback + AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt. All 9 unit tests pass.
description:
Implement libs/core/src/lib/crypto.ts with pure functions only. No side effects, no storage, no browser APIs except Web Crypto.

Functions:
- deriveKey(password: string, salt: Uint8Array): Promise<CryptoKey>
  - Uses Argon2id via hash-wasm with hashLength: 32 (bytes) for AES-256-GCM compatibility
  - Falls back to PBKDF2-SHA256 (Web Crypto) if WASM unavailable; records kdf: 'pbkdf2'
  - Argon2id params: m=65536 (64MB), t=3 iterations, p=1 (OWASP minimum)
- encrypt(key: CryptoKey, plaintext: string): Promise<{ iv: Uint8Array; ciphertext: ArrayBuffer }>
  - AES-256-GCM via Web Crypto
- decrypt(key: CryptoKey, iv: Uint8Array, ciphertext: ArrayBuffer): Promise<string>
- generateSalt(): Uint8Array  // 16 random bytes
- generateIV(): Uint8Array    // 12 random bytes

Add hash-wasm to package.json.

Unit tests (crypto.spec.ts) must cover:
- Encrypt→decrypt roundtrip produces original plaintext
- Different salts produce different keys for the same password
- Wrong password fails to decrypt (GCM tag authentication error)
- Tampered ciphertext fails to decrypt
- Salt is 16 bytes, IV is 12 bytes

Validate WASM availability in Firefox MV3 service workers early — PBKDF2 fallback must work if WASM is restricted.
title: Phase 2 Step 2 — Crypto module

close_reason: Scaffolded libs/core (scope:core) and libs/shared (scope:shared) with @nx/js. Updated depConstraints in root .eslintrc.json to enforce app→core/shared, core→shared, shared→nothing. nx lint passes across all 3 projects.
description: Generate placeholder libs/core and libs/shared libraries using @nx/js. Configure @nx/enforce-module-boundaries ESLint rule to enforce that apps can import from libs, but libs/shared cannot import from libs/core, etc. Establishes the import boundary architecture before any real code is written.
title: Scaffold empty libs/core and libs/shared with module boundary rules

close_reason: Implemented vault.ts with createEntry/updateEntry/deleteEntry/serializeVault/deserializeVault (manual type guard validation). All 14 unit tests pass.
description:
Implement libs/core/src/lib/vault.ts with pure functions for vault CRUD. Operates on the decrypted VaultEntry[] array in memory. No side effects, no browser APIs.

Functions:
- createEntry(entries: VaultEntry[], data: NewEntry): VaultEntry[]
  - Generates id via crypto.randomUUID()
  - Sets createdAt and updatedAt to current ISO timestamp
- updateEntry(entries: VaultEntry[], updated: VaultEntry): VaultEntry[]
  - Updates updatedAt; throws if id not found
- deleteEntry(entries: VaultEntry[], id: string): VaultEntry[]
  - Throws if id not found
- serializeVault(entries: VaultEntry[]): string  // JSON.stringify
- deserializeVault(json: string): VaultEntry[]
  - JSON.parse + manual type guard validation
  - Check required fields and correct types on each entry
  - Throw on invalid structure — do not silently return garbage

Unit tests (vault.spec.ts) must cover: add, update, delete, not-found update/delete, serialize/deserialize roundtrip.
title: Phase 2 Step 3 — Vault module

close_reason: Scaffolded apps/browser-extension-e2e/project.json with e2e target. @nx/playwright installed and registered in nx.json. Chromium installed via playwright install chromium. install-browsers Nx target added to e2e project so browser install runs automatically as a dependsOn step — no postinstall script needed. nx show project browser-extension-e2e lists e2e target.
description: Install @nx/playwright via nx add. Scaffold apps/browser-extension-e2e/ using the @nx/playwright:configuration generator. Install Chromium binary via playwright install. Add e2e entry to nx.json targetDefaults for caching. Add .tmp/ and playwright output paths to .gitignore. Acceptance: nx show project browser-extension-e2e lists an e2e target.
title: Scaffold Playwright E2E project

close_reason: Nx 22.5.1 workspace initialized with ts preset. apps/ and libs/ dirs created. npm install complete. .gitignore updated.
description: Run nx init or create-nx-workspace with the ts preset inside the existing repo. Target structure: apps/, libs/. This is the foundation all other setup tasks depend on.
title: Initialize Nx workspace (ts preset) inside current repo

close_reason: Implemented background/index.ts with full message bus, state machine (UNINITIALIZED/LOCKED/UNLOCKED), auto-lock timer (5min, resets on authenticated messages only), and service worker restart recovery. Build passes.
description:
Implement apps/browser-extension/src/background/index.ts. This is the single source of truth for vault state; the popup is purely a view.

State machine:
  UNINITIALIZED → (SETUP) → LOCKED → (UNLOCK) → UNLOCKED
                                  ↑                   |
                                  └──── (LOCK/timeout) ┘

Startup:
1. Call isConfigured() → set state to UNINITIALIZED or LOCKED
2. Register browser.runtime.onMessage listener

Message handlers:
- GET_STATUS → return { type: 'STATUS'; state } — does NOT reset auto-lock timer
- SETUP → deriveKey, encrypt empty vault, writeVault, transition to UNLOCKED
- UNLOCK → readVault, deriveKey, attempt decrypt; success → UNLOCKED, failure → { type: 'ERROR' }
- LOCK → clear vaultKey and entries in memory, transition to LOCKED — does NOT reset auto-lock timer
- GET_ENTRIES → return in-memory entries (or LOCKED if not unlocked) — resets auto-lock timer
- ADD_ENTRY / UPDATE_ENTRY / DELETE_ENTRY → mutate entries, re-encrypt, writeVault — resets auto-lock timer

Auto-lock:
- setTimeout, default 5 minutes (hardcoded constant — configurable in Phase 4)
- Reset only on authenticated messages (GET_ENTRIES, ADD_ENTRY, UPDATE_ENTRY, DELETE_ENTRY)
- LOCK and GET_STATUS do not reset the timer
- On expiry: dispatch internal LOCK

Service worker restart behavior:
- MV3 SWs can be terminated at any time; on restart vaultKey is null
- State re-derived from isConfigured(); vault exists → LOCKED
- Popup receives LOCKED on any request, re-routes to UnlockView
- No special handling needed — the existing flow covers this
title: Phase 2 Step 5 — Background service worker

close_reason: ESLint + Prettier configured at workspace root. @nx/eslint, @nx/eslint-plugin, @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin all installed. @nx/enforce-module-boundaries wired. nx lint passes.
description: Wire up ESLint and Prettier via Nx's built-in tooling (@nx/eslint). Configure workspace-root-level rules including @nx/enforce-module-boundaries to establish import boundaries from day one. Should be done before any application code is written.
title: Configure ESLint + Prettier at workspace root

close_reason: Created libs/shared/src/lib/types.ts with all Phase 2 types (LoginEntry, SecureNoteEntry, VaultEntry, NewEntry, EncryptedVault, VaultRequest, VaultResponse). Updated index.ts to re-export. Build passes.
title: Phase 2 Step 1 — Shared types

close_reason: Scaffolded apps/browser-extension with @nx/react, Vite, TypeScript, ESLint. nx lint and nx build both pass.
description: Use @nx/vite and @nx/react to generate the browser-extension app inside apps/. Configure TypeScript, set up the Vite config skeleton, and confirm nx build browser-extension runs without errors.
title: Create apps/browser-extension Nx app with Vite + React + TypeScript

close_reason: Implemented IStorageProvider interface and LocalStorageProvider wrapping browser.storage.local. All 6 unit tests pass with mocked storage.
description:
Implement the IStorageProvider interface and LocalStorageProvider in libs/core/src/lib/storage/.

IStorageProvider interface (IStorageProvider.ts):
  readVault(): Promise<EncryptedVault | null>
  writeVault(vault: EncryptedVault): Promise<void>
  isConfigured(): Promise<boolean>  // async — project-plan.md shows sync but async is correct
  configure(options?: unknown): Promise<void>
  displayName: string

LocalStorageProvider (LocalStorageProvider.ts):
- Wraps browser.storage.local via webextension-polyfill
- Stores vault JSON under fixed key 'ppm_vault'
- configure() is a no-op (no configuration needed for local storage)

Unit tests (LocalStorageProvider.spec.ts) use a mock of browser.storage.local. Cover: readVault when empty, readVault with data, writeVault, isConfigured true/false.
title: Phase 2 Step 4 — Storage layer

description: Install web-ext as a devDependency. Add a firefox-compat Nx target to apps/browser-extension/package.json using nx:run-commands executor (web-ext lint --source-dir=apps/browser-extension/dist --warnings-as-errors), with dependsOn: [build]. Add e2e, e2e:ci, and e2e:firefox-compat scripts to root package.json. Acceptance: npm run e2e:firefox-compat runs web-ext lint against dist/ and exits 0.
title: Add Firefox compatibility check via web-ext

close_reason: Ran nx migrate latest — bumped Nx packages from 22.5.1 to 22.5.4, resolving all minimatch high severity vulns. Remaining 21 vulns (3 low, 10 moderate, 8 high) are in transitive dev dependencies (jsdom, @swc/cli, koa via @nx/module-federation) that require breaking changes to fix and do not ship in the extension bundle.
description: 5 high severity vulnerabilities exist in minimatch via Nx 22.5.1. Before modifying any package versions manually, use 'nx migrate' first to let Nx manage its own version upgrades and migration scripts correctly. Only fall back to manual version edits if nx migrate cannot resolve the issue.
title: Address npm audit vulnerabilities in Nx dependencies

description:
Implement the popup React UI in apps/browser-extension/src/popup/. No external state management library — use useState/useReducer + React context.

App.tsx:
- On mount, send GET_STATUS to background worker
- Render based on STATUS response:
  state === 'UNINITIALIZED' → <SetupView />
  state === 'LOCKED'        → <UnlockView />
  state === 'UNLOCKED'      → <VaultView />

SetupView.tsx:
- Two password inputs (password + confirm)
- Submit disabled until both fields match; inline error if they diverge
- Optional hint field (stores null if blank — hint UI surfaced in Phase 4)
- On submit: send SETUP message
- Warning: 'If you forget your master password and have not set up a recovery key, your vault cannot be recovered. Recovery key setup is available in Phase 4 settings.'

UnlockView.tsx:
- Single password input
- On submit: send UNLOCK message
- Show inline error on wrong password

VaultView.tsx:
- Entry list grouped by type (login / note)
- 'Add Entry' button → type picker (Login / Secure Note) → EntryForm in create mode
- Clicking an existing entry → EntryForm in edit mode
- Per-entry delete button
- Lock button → send LOCK → return to UnlockView

EntryForm.tsx (shared form, switches on entry type):
- Login fields: title, URL, username, password (with show/hide toggle), notes
- Note fields: title, body

EntryList.tsx:
- Scrollable list of vault entries grouped by type

Files to create:
- popup/App.tsx (rewrite)
- popup/views/SetupView.tsx
- popup/views/UnlockView.tsx
- popup/views/VaultView.tsx
- popup/components/EntryForm.tsx
- popup/components/EntryList.tsx
title: Phase 2 Step 6 — Popup UI

close_reason: Installed husky and lint-staged. Pre-commit hook runs lint-staged (prettier --write + eslint --fix on JS/TS files, prettier --write on JSON/MD/CSS/HTML). prepare script ensures husky is set up on npm install.
description: Install Husky and lint-staged. Configure a pre-commit hook that runs Prettier (--write) and ESLint (--fix) against all staged files. Auto-fix issues where possible; block the commit if unfixable errors remain.
title: Add Husky and lint-staged for pre-commit hooks

description:
Extend apps/browser-extension-e2e with Playwright tests for the vault flows. Create apps/browser-extension-e2e/src/vault.spec.ts.

Tests to implement:
- Setup flow: first launch shows setup screen; create master password; lands on vault list
- Unlock flow: after reload, shows unlock screen; wrong password shows error; correct password unlocks
- Add LoginEntry: click Add Entry → pick Login type → fill form → verify entry appears in list
- Add SecureNoteEntry: click Add Entry → pick Secure Note type → fill form → verify entry appears in list
- Persist across reload: reload extension popup, unlock, verify entry still present
- Delete entry: delete an entry, verify it is removed from the list
- Service worker restart: simulate SW termination mid-session (e.g., close/reopen); verify popup re-prompts for master password rather than crashing

Run with: npm run e2e
title: Phase 2 Step 7 — E2E tests
